channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI shifts its focus to business users amid Anthropic pressure
item.description: The same ChatGPT chatbot that gave OpenAI’s chief financial officer Sarah Friar a tilapia recipe for a recent Sunday night dinner at home is also now doing her most mundane tasks at work like summarizing her emails and Slack messages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Why AI is the ultimate accelerator for creativity
item.description: Have you noticed that in the current discourse around artificial intelligence, the narrative often slips into one of two extremes? There is either a techno-utopian dream of total automation or a dystopian nightmare where human agency is erased.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Nixon, Fast Company

item.title: AI anxiety is turning volatile
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week via email here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Google DeepMind’s Demis Hassabis on the long game of AI
item.description: In 1988, a London pre-teen with a penchant for programming and gaming wrote a version of the classic board game Othello—also known as Reversi—for his Amiga 500 home computer. Teaching a piece of software to play the game was an ambitious coding project for someone so young.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: AI isn’t built for all languages and cultures. There’s a push to fix that
item.description: Egyptian coder Assem Sabry has long wanted an AI model that represents his culture. The problem is he hasn’t been able to find one. “The AI industry in Egypt . . . doesn’t exist,” Sabry says. So he built his own: Horus, named after the ancient Egyptian god of the sky.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Ease into Azure Kubernetes Application Network
item.description: If you’re using Kubernetes, especially a managed version like Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), you don’t need to think about the underlying hardware. All you need to do is build your application and it should run, its containers managed by the service’s orchestrator. At least that’s the theory.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Salesforce launches Headless 360 to support agent‑first enterprise workflows
item.description: Salesforce is packaging its developer and AI tooling, including its vibe coding environment Agentforce Vibes, into a new platform named Headless 360, designed to help enterprise teams build agent-first workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI pulls out of a second Stargate data center deal
item.description: In the space of one week, OpenAI has pulled out of two European Stargate data center deals, one in the UK and the second in Norway.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: New findings from this Gallup poll show how Americans are using AI for health advice
item.description: When Tiffany Davis has a question about a symptom from the weight-loss injections she’s taking, she doesn’t call her doctor. She pulls out her phone and consults ChatGPT. “I’ll just basically let ChatGPT know my status, how I’m feeling,” said the 42-year-old in Mesquite, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: The idea that the internet is built for people is crumbling. That has huge implications for your business
item.description: For years, companies have assumed the internet was built for people. Websites were designed to attract human attention, explain, persuade, reassure, and eventually convert.
item.dc:creator: Enrique Dans, Fast Company

item.title: ChatGPT maker OpenAI shifts its focus to business users amid Anthropic pressure
item.description: OpenAI executives say they will introduce a new artificial intelligence model for “high-value professional work” as the company faces heightened competition with rival Anthropic in attracting corporate customers to adopt AI assistants in their workplaces.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rivian’s factory will soon be powered partly by old batteries from its own EVs
item.description: At its factory in Illinois, Rivian will soon use more than 100 retired EV batteries in an on-site power system that will help it save money on electric bills.
item.dc:creator: Adele Peters, Fast Company

item.title: Blancco confirms Mac adoption is accelerating
item.description: While sales of new Macs are surging the second-user market is also seeing strong momentum, prompting Blancco Technology Group and Cambrionix to introduce a new solution to help quickly erase and prepare large numbers of Macs for sale.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to create your own custom Android air gesture
item.description: Psst: Come close. I’ve got something to share with you, and I don’t want everyone around here to hear it. Oh — hi! Sorry, I didn’t realize you were here.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tap into the AI APIs of Google Chrome and Microsoft Edge
item.description: With every passing year, local AI models get smaller, more efficient, and more comparable in power with their higher-end, cloud-hosted counterparts. You can run many of the same inference jobs on your own hardware, without needing an internet connection or even a particularly powerful GPU.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Satellite backhaul service Globalstar has a new, rich owner amid challenging market conditions
item.description: Globalstar, a mobile satellite services (MSS) operator in which Apple has a 20% stake, on Tuesday announced a merger agreement with Amazon, which, pending regulatory approval, could soon bring direct to device services (D2D) services to Leo, the latter’s low Earth orbit satellite network.
item.dc:creator: Network World
